entities.name: idol
entities.raw_quote: which, when consecrated by the priestly adept who had already established communication with them, might become the special abode of some spirit

entities.name: spirit communication
entities.raw_quote: It is, as before remarked, a difficult thing to establish communication with the spirits, but when once established, 'place is given to them' (Eph. iv. 27), and they are loth to surrender the power

entities.name: evil spirit
entities.raw_quote: when men give themselves over to such exceptional wickedness, it is implied... that an evil spirit enters into them and possesses them

entities.name: image worship development
entities.raw_quote: The development of image worship seems to have been gradual... at first... regarded only as symbols... then sacred, and eventually as special forms or manifestations

entities.name: Babylonian king
entities.raw_quote: the worship of the dead Babylonian king under similar forms

entities.name: Belus (Marduk)
entities.raw_quote: There was a golden image of the Sun in the temple of Belus at Babylon

entities.name: Cuzco deity (Sun god)
entities.raw_quote: a similar image of gold was found in the temple of Cuzco, in Peru

entities.name: Baal (Sun god)
entities.raw_quote: Brilliant metal reflectors, or 'Sun Images,' were placed over the altars of Baal, the Sun god of the Canaanites

entities.name: Osiris
entities.raw_quote: the dead were always spoken of as 'in Osiris'

entities.name: tonsure

attributes.practiced_by: Arabians, priests of Osiris, Pagan Romans, Buddhist monks

entities.raw_quote: The ceremony of tonsure... was an old practice of the priests of Mithra, who in their tonsures imitated the solar disk

entities.name: nimbus (aureola)
entities.raw_quote: The nimbus was also commonly placed... round those of the Roman Emperors, to whom, after death, divine honours were paid

entities.name: Sacred Heart
entities.raw_quote: the word for 'heart' was >v 'Bel', and on the principle of using words with a double meaning... the heart became thus a symbol of the god

entities.name: bulla
entities.raw_quote: The Roman youth wore a golden ornament suspended from their necks, called the 'bulla'... heart-shaped... an especial symbol of the god

entities.name: Dionysius Eleuthereus (Bacchus)

attributes.myth: torn to pieces, heart preserved by Minerva, regenerated

entities.raw_quote: When he was torn to pieces, his heart was preserved by Minerva, and 'by a new regeneration again emerged... afterwards filled up the number of the gods'

entities.name: human sacrifice
entities.raw_quote: These victims... were usually confined to captives taken in war, slaves and criminals

entities.name: cross as altar

attributes.sacrificial_death: symbolic of giving over to pagan god

entities.raw_quote: death on the cross... was a sacrificial death, the cross being the altar of the god

entities.name: goddess (Mylitta/Ceres/Isis)
entities.raw_quote: She was known as 'Mylitta,' 'The Mediatrix,' in Babylon... There were no bloody sacrifices allowed on her altars

entities.name: round cake (Popana/Mola)
entities.raw_quote: The thin round cake... occurs on all altars... symbol... of the Sun, and of his Son

entities.name: Sraddha

attributes.practices: donations of cattle, land, gold, silver

entities.raw_quote: In India, in the services of 'The Sraddha' for the repose of the dead, it is urged that 'donations of cattle, land, gold and silver and other things' should be given

entities.name: Gurgumi
entities.raw_quote: 'The Gurgumi, or prayers for the dead, are very expensive'

entities.name: Telete (mysterious sacrifice)

attributes.purpose: for sins of living and dead

entities.raw_quote: This, according to Plato... was offered for the sins of the living and dead, and was supposed to free them from all the evils

entities.name: Purgatory

attributes.function: allowed for posthumous intercession via sacrifices

entities.raw_quote: The supposed existence of a Purgatory suggested... the possibility of appealing the anger of the gods by costly sacrifices made by friends

entities.name: self-mortification (penances)

attributes.practiced_by: Egyptians at Isis feast, Callimachus' sailors, Baal priests, Corybantes, Fakirs, Sunayases

entities.raw_quote: The Egyptians... scourged themselves... The priests of Baal... cut themselves... with lancets until the blood gushed out

entities.name: holy water
entities.raw_quote: Holy water was also used to sprinkle the dead, and to purify houses and temples

summary: This chunk explores the theological and ritual foundations of image worship, spirit habitation, and ancestor/ghost veneration in ancient pagan traditions. It traces how objects (idols, images, symbols) were believed to become inhabited by spirits or deities through consecration, and discusses practices such as human sacrifice, self-mortification, holy water rites, and funeral offerings—including the round cake sacrifices to goddesses like Ceres/Isis. The text links these to later Christian adaptations and draws on classical sources (Virgil, Plato), biblical references, and comparative ethnography (India, Mexico, Babylon). While not explicitly about medieval European necromancy, it provides background for later magical traditions by detailing ancient spirit-invocation mechanics.
key_passages: which, when consecrated by the priestly adept who had already established communication with them, might become the special abode of some spirit
It is, as before remarked, a difficult thing to establish communication with the spirits, but when once established, 'place is given to them' (Eph. iv. 27), and they are loth to surrender the power
The development of image worship seems to have been gradual... at first... regarded only as symbols... then sacred, and eventually as special forms or manifestations
There were no bloody sacrifices allowed on her altars... and the usual offering was a round cake, the symbol of the Sun
In Egypt... the priests induced the people to expend large sums on the celebration of funeral rites. For, besides the embalming process, the tomb itself was purchased at an immense expense
